source:
#Step 1: Read Silver Tables

from pyspark.sql.functions import col, when, lit

# Read Silver tables
emp_df = spark.table("hranalytics_db.silver_employee")
perf_df = spark.table("hranalytics_db.silver_performance")
survey_df = spark.table("hranalytics_db.silver_survey")
exit_df = spark.table("hranalytics_db.silver_exit")

#Step 2: Read Current Dimension (SCD2)
#Always use only current records
dim_df = spark.table("hranalytics_db.dim_employee") \
              .filter(col("is_current") == True)

#Step 3: Join All Sources
fact_df = dim_df.alias("d") \
    .join(perf_df.alias("p"), col("d.emp_id") == col("p.emp_id"), "left") \
    .join(survey_df.alias("s"), col("d.emp_id") == col("s.emp_id"), "left") \
    .join(exit_df.alias("e"), col("d.emp_id") == col("e.emp_id"), "left")


#Step 4: Derive Metrics Columns

fact_df = fact_df.select(
    col("d.emp_id").alias("emp_sk"),
    col("p.rating"),
    col("s.survey_score"),
    col("p.review_date"),
    col("s.survey_date"),
    col("e.exit_date"),
    
    # Attrition Flag
    when(col("e.emp_id").isNotNull(), lit(1)).otherwise(lit(0)).alias("attrition_flag"),
    
    # Engagement Bucket
    when(col("s.survey_score") >= 8, "High")
    .when(col("s.survey_score") >= 5, "Medium")
    .otherwise("Low").alias("engagement_level"),
    
    # Risk Flag (Early Churn Detection)
    when((col("p.rating") <= 2) & (col("s.survey_score") <= 5), 1)
    .otherwise(0).alias("churn_risk_flag")
)

#Step 5: Handle Duplicates (Important)

#If multiple records per employee (e.g., multiple reviews)

from pyspark.sql.window import Window
from pyspark.sql.functions import row_number

window_spec = Window.partitionBy("emp_sk").orderBy(col("review_date").desc())

fact_df = fact_df.withColumn("rn", row_number().over(window_spec)) \
                 .filter(col("rn") == 1) \
                 .drop("rn")

#Step 6: Write to Gold Layer
fact_df.write.mode("overwrite").saveAsTable("hranalytics_db.fact_employee_metrics")
